explicit_change_request: DELIB-FAB11-REMEDIATION-20260610B / PAUTH-FAB11-20260610: amend GOV-12 to recognize pytest execution and pytest coverage mappings as current governed verification evidence.
full_content:
Creating a work item initiates test creation; the backlog initiates implementation. Work items describe implementation work and originate from three sources: bugs/defects discovered during testing, owner-provided requirements, or Claude-proposed improvements. Creating a work item does NOT initiate execution of the implementation — only the backlog triggers execution. Creating a work item DOES trigger the creation of a test. The test ensures that the implementation of the work item satisfies the specification. Test forms: (1) Logical assertion — exists/does not exist, greater than/less than, if-then. (2) User story — a verifiable process description. (3) Abstract description — measurements, pseudocode, or other information describing the desired implementation.

FAB-11 pytest evidence amendment (2026-06-12): work-item creation still initiates test planning, but current repository-native pytest files and their execution output are governed verification evidence. Historical MemBase `tests` rows imported from Agent Red before application isolation are app-scoped history and must not be counted as live pass evidence unless refreshed by a current execution record.
